detail: GWAS Catalog association with p=3e-10. Effect: OR/Beta=0.06667826 [0.046-0.087] SD units decrease. Initial sample: 21,907 European ancestry individuals. Replication sample: NA.
whyItMatters: This common-variant association is matched locally against the uploaded genotype and should be treated as a tendency signal, not a prediction.
notes: Generated from bulk GWAS Catalog data; effect size and ancestry transferability require review.

title: RYR1 / desflurane
summary: Patients with the rs28933397 CC genotype may have a decreased, but not absent, risk for malignant hyperthermia based on this variant when treated with volatile anesthetics (desflurane, enflurane, halothane, isoflurane, methoxyflurane, sevoflurane) and/or succinylcholine as compared to patients with the TT or CT genotype. Other genetic or clinical factors may also influence the risk for malignant hyperthermia.
riskSummary: RYR1 variant associated with toxicity for desflurane
detail: Patients with the rs28933397 CC genotype may have a decreased, but not absent, risk for malignant hyperthermia based on this variant when treated with volatile anesthetics (desflurane, enflurane, halothane, isoflurane, methoxyflurane, sevoflurane) and/or succinylcholine as compared to patients with the TT or CT genotype. Other genetic or clinical factors may also influence the risk for malignant hyperthermia.
whyItMatters: PharmGKB curates pharmacogenomic evidence from the literature, with level 1A–2B annotations backed by expert review and published clinical studies.
notes: PharmGKB annotations reflect published literature and should not replace clinical pharmacogenomic testing.

title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the rs121908757 AA genotype (do not have a copy of the CFTR S549R variant) and cystic fibrosis have an unknown response to ivacaftor treatment, as response may depend on the presence of other CFTR variants. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.

title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the rs121908757 AC genotype (one copy of the CFTR S549R variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. FDA-approved drug labeling information and CPIC guidelines indicate use of ivacaftor in cystic fibrosis patients with at least one copy of S549R. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.

title: A case-control study of Chinese Han patients reported that the rs2243093 (C;C) genotype has a...
summary: A case-control study of Chinese Han patients reported that the rs2243093 (C;C) genotype has an increase risk for coronary heart disease. A meta-analysis was performed and found that there is a strong association with risk of ischemic stroke, however, the authors suggest that this polymorphism may actually be linked with another (unknown) locus that is the culprit responsible for the increase in ischemic stroke risk.

summary: GLUT1 deficiency syndrome 1? Feedback sought ...
